title: Encoding Binary Numbers
description:
A binary number can be encoded into decimal digits by the following counting method.

Binary number Counting Decimal digits
000 3x0 30
0111 1x0 3x1 1031
111000001 3x1 5x0 1x1 315011
1010 1x1 1x0 1x1 1x0 11101110
input: The input includes lines of a character string representing a binary number. The length of a binary number in each line is between 1 and 100 inclusive. The line containing only zero (0) indicates the end of the input. The last line must not be encoded into decimal digits. The input may contain up to 1,000 lines.
output: Print out in each line the decimal digits corresponding to the binary number. There is no space between digits.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Asia Pacific, Thailand, Thailand Southern Programming Contest, Thailand Southern Programming Contest 2011
